text: This PR addresses 46 performance audit findings across 34 frontend files. The majority of fixes are correctly applied and well-executed — dynamic imports, useMemo wrap, transition scoping, and Image optimization are all done properly. However, two critical regressions were introduced: ProtectedRoute.tsx uses string .includes() for role matching (substring bug), and TierPathVisualizer.tsx's scaleX fill bar is invisible due to missing explicit width. The chart barrel imports and AttendanceChecklist callback memoization are partially addressed.

reasoning: Two regression bugs need fixing: substring role matching in ProtectedRoute (allows ORGANIZATION_ADMIN to match ADMIN) and invisible fill bar in TierPathVisualizer (absolutely positioned div missing width). Both are straightforward fixes. Recharts barrel imports should also be switched to tree-shakeable sub-paths.

message: onUpdate callback correctly wrapped in useCallback with [onChange] dep, and ToolbarButton extracted as module-level memo() component with stable useCallback refs for all toolbar actions — fully addresses all three audit findings in this file.
ThemeToggleButton correctly imported from @/components/theme/ThemeToggle instead of being defined inline inside TopNav, eliminating the unmount/remount cycle on every render.

watch() calls correctly migrated from blanket watch() to per-field watch() calls with individual useMemo dependencies, fixing both the stale object reference and the unnecessary re-render on every field change.
EventSeriesForm correctly imported via next/dynamic with ssr:false, preventing ~440 lines of form logic from being bundled when showRecurringOption is false.
RichTextEditor correctly imported via next/dynamic with ssr:false, lazy-loading 80-100KB of tiptap only when the blog form is opened.
Namespace import import * as Sentry correctly replaced with named import import { captureException }, enabling tree-shaking. Relative import also fixed to use @/lib/api path alias.
users.find() correctly moved from inline IIFE to useMemo with [users, openMenu] deps, with selectedUser guard on the rendering, eliminating O(n) search on every render.
Mobile menu correctly changed from conditional mount {mobileOpen && ...} to Tailwind hidden/block class toggle, keeping the DOM mounted and avoiding React mount/unmount cost on each toggle.
O(n²) levels.find() inside levels.map() correctly factored out — currentTier computed once before the map, replacing the duplicate linear lookups with a single pass.
Date formatting correctly moved from inline new Date().toLocaleDateString() to useMemo with [orgs] dependency, avoiding repeated work on every render.
Date formatting correctly moved from inline in JSX to useMemo with [data] dependency, preventing repeated date parsing on every render for large lists.
onSuccess callback correctly uses the variables parameter instead of the potentially stale reviewMutation.variables closure reference.
IIFE for icon rendering correctly extracted to const IconComponent before the return statement, eliminating function creation on every render.
React key correctly changed from stat.label to `${stat.label}-${idx}`, preventing key collisions if two stat cards share the same label.
addCertification correctly guarded with .includes() check to prevent duplicate certifications before pushing.
Separate type and value imports from @/lib/shared correctly combined into a single import statement.
toggle function correctly wrapped in useCallback with [setTheme, resolvedTheme] deps, preventing recreation on every render.

rolesKey.includes(user.role) uses String.prototype.includes() for substring matching instead of Array.prototype.includes() for exact role comparison. A user with role 'ADMIN' would incorrectly pass a check for 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN' because 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN' contains 'ADMIN' as a substring. This is a security regression that can grant users access to routes they shouldn't have.
suggestion: Replace rolesKey.includes(user.role) with allowedRoles.includes(user.role) and add allowedRoles to the useEffect dependency array. The original performance concern (effect re-running on inline array) is less severe than this correctness bug, or use a stable reference via useMemo at call sites.

message: The connection fill bar is position: absolute with left:0 but no explicit width (no right-0 or w-full). For absolutely positioned non-replaced elements, width: auto computes to shrink-to-fit — zero for an empty <div>. scaleX(completionPct/100) of zero is always zero, making the progress bar completely invisible regardless of completion percentage. The original width: X% worked because percentage width on absolute elements is relative to the containing block.
suggestion: Add w-full to the className or add right-0 so the absolutely positioned div has measurable width for scaleX() to operate on.

message: Still imports recharts components from the top-level barrel (from 'recharts') instead of tree-shakeable sub-path imports (e.g., from 'recharts/es6/pie/Pie'). While next/dynamic is correctly applied at consumer pages, the component itself still pulls in the entire recharts barrel.
suggestion: Import from tree-shakeable sub-paths: import { PieChart } from 'recharts/es6/chart/PieChart', import { Pie } from 'recharts/es6/pie/Pie', etc., or verify that the bundler (next.js webpack/turbopack) can tree-shake barrel imports. Recharts v2+ may not support sub-path imports — if not, document why the barrel is acceptable.

message: Same barrel import issue as CategoryPieChart — recharts components imported from 'recharts' barrel instead of tree-shakeable sub-paths.
suggestion: Apply the same tree-shaking treatment as CategoryPieChart, or verify bundler behavior and document if barrel is required.

message: VolunteerRow is correctly extracted as a memo() component, but all props passed to it (onToggle, onHoursChange, onRatingChange, onApprove) are inline arrow functions created on every render of AttendanceChecklist. This means memo()'s shallow comparison always detects new props, so every row re-renders on any state change — completely defeating the purpose of memoization.
suggestion: Wrap each callback in useCallback with proper dependencies. For onApprove which captures the volunteer object, use a data attribute or pass the volunteerId to handleApprove instead of closing over v in the map.

message: Notification polling has staleTime: 30000 equal to refetchInterval: 30000. With TanStack Query, when staleTime equals refetchInterval, the data becomes stale at exactly the same moment the refetch fires, so it still fetches every 30s. The audit specifically flagged the persistent network request as wasteful — this configuration doesn't prevent any refetches.
suggestion: Increase staleTime (e.g., staleTime: 60000) so data remains fresh when the refetch interval fires, or use document.visibilitychange to pause polling when the tab is backgrounded. Alternatively, refetchInterval can be removed entirely if staleTime + refetchOnWindowFocus provides sufficient freshness.

message: The audit fix for 19 named Lucide icon imports was not applied. However, the current approach (importing exactly the 19 icons used) is actually more tree-shakeable than the suggested dynamic registry (import * as Icons from 'lucide-react'), which would bundle all icons. The current code is acceptable and arguably optimal.
suggestion: No action needed — the current named imports are tree-shakeable and only bundle the icons actually referenced.

message: hoursValue is typed as string, but the code checks hoursValue !== undefined (lines 149, 157, 167, 178). A string variable initialized with '' or '0' will never be undefined at runtime — this is a dead comparison that always evaluates to true.
suggestion: Remove the !== undefined checks and just check hoursValue !== '' since the initial value is ''.
